Title: Biosynthetic Origin of the Methoxy Group in Quinine and Related Alkaloids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: methoxylated and non-methoxylated Cinchona alkaloid intermediates (e.g., quinine/quinidine-related pathway products)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is an antimalarial drug and bittering ingredient; quinidine is an antiarrhythmic medication
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens for quinine/quinidine and dihydro analogs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves metabolic flux from strictosidine and 10-methoxystrictosidine to downstream alkaloid products and shifts product distribution toward methoxylated products in heterologous reconstitution
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: identified as an ortholog in the C. pubescens transcriptome; detailed tissue pattern NA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-infiltration of tryptamine and secologanin in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome mining from C. pubescens; accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without transporter, strictosidine and 10-methoxystrictosidine accumulate and downstream flux is poor; broader tradeoffs NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstitution and metabolic engineering to improve flux and tune methoxylated/non-methoxylated alkaloid ratios by adding transport capacity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates that transport can strongly control product distribution in both native and heterologous systems
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpSTTr is a C. pubescens ortholog of the Catharanthus strictosidine transporter. 2. Co-expression of CpSTTr with pathway enzymes in N. benthamiana improved conversion of strictosidine and 10-methoxystrictosidine into downstream products. 3. Inclusion of CpSTTr increased relative accumulation of methoxylated downstream products, highlighting transporter control over pathway flux.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: quinine/Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis; monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for methoxylated alkaloids including quinine and quinidine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key early control step for methoxylated branch establishment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine and related methoxylated Cinchona alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens for major methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of serotonin and thereby initiates biosynthesis of 5-methoxytryptamine-derived methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate infiltration; LC-MS detection of serotonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: tryptamine infiltration into N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome mining from C. pubescens; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering methoxylated Cinchona alkaloid production when combined with CpOMT1 and downstream pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two highly similar enzymes (97.9% amino acid identity) producing serotonin from tryptamine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpT5H1 was identified by homology to rice CYP71P1 and converts tryptamine to serotonin in planta. 2. Its activity supports early installation of the methoxy-group precursor before strictosidine formation. 3. Together with CpOMT1, it enables the methoxylated alkaloid branch.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: quinine/Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis; monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for methoxylated alkaloids including quinine and quinidine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key early control step for methoxylated branch establishment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine and related methoxylated Cinchona alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens for major methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of serotonin and thereby initiates biosynthesis of 5-methoxytryptamine-derived methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate infiltration; LC-MS detection of serotonin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: tryptamine infiltration into N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome mining from C. pubescens; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering methoxylated Cinchona alkaloid production when combined with CpOMT1 and downstream pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Second highly similar tryptamine 5-hydroxylase candidate active in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpT5H2 was identified by homology and shown to hydroxylate tryptamine to serotonin in N. benthamiana. 2. It has a complementary tissue expression pattern relative to CpT5H1. 3. It contributes to early precursor formation for methoxylated Cinchona alkaloids.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the hydroxyl group of serotonin to produce 5-methoxytryptamine
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: quinine/Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis; monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the methoxylated tryptamine precursor used to make methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-establishing step; native preferred substrate indicates primary pathway role
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: O-methyltransferase; evolutionarily related to aromatic O-methyltransferases from other monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine and related methoxylated Cinchona alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens for major methoxylated alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 5-methoxytryptamine from serotonin, thereby installing the methoxy group precursor for methoxylated alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay with purified protein from E. coli; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS comparison with authentic standard
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in all tissues considered (FPKM > 4) and co-expressed with CpT5H1 or CpT5H2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; recombinant protein expressed in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: substrate feeding/infiltration with serotonin or co-expression with pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome mining/RNA-seq from C. pubescens; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can also methylate N-acetyl-serotonin and weakly methylate 6-hydroxycinchoninone, indicating some substrate promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A crucial enzyme for engineering methoxylated alkaloid production; should be combined with CpT5H and downstream promiscuous enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously annotated as 6'-hydroxycinchoninone O-methyltransferase, but this study reclassifies it as serotonin O-methyltransferase based on substrate preference and in planta activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among seven candidates, only CpOMT1 methylated serotonin to 5-methoxytryptamine. 2. CpOMT1 converted serotonin much more efficiently than 6-hydroxycinchoninone, indicating serotonin is the native substrate. 3. This enzyme defines the early methoxylation step feeding methoxylated quinine-related alkaloid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tryptamine or substituted tryptamines with secologanin to form strictosidine or methoxy/hydroxy strictosidine analogs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: quinine/Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis; monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry step into strictosidine-derived alkaloid scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate pathway intermediate that channels both methoxy and non-methoxy branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: committed-step enzyme for branch entry; substrate tolerance is a key control feature
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine and related methoxylated/non-methoxylated Cinchona alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens for major alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Accepts both methoxy and non-methoxy tryptamine substrates, enabling parallel formation of methoxylated and non-methoxylated alkaloid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization in N. benthamiana; substrate feeding with multiple amines; LC-MS metabolite profiling during pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: likely vacuole-associated product context; enzyme localization NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome mining from C. pubescens; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In heterologous N. benthamiana, products may accumulate upstream without transporter-assisted export
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable for synthetic biology because it accommodates substituted tryptamines that many known STR orthologs do not accept
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically distant from CrSTR and RsSTR; this divergence may underlie unusual substrate promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpSTR was identified because known STR orthologs generally do not accept C5-substituted tryptamines. 2. CpSTR accepts tryptamine, 5-methoxytryptamine, and 5-hydroxytryptamine, unlike canonical orthologs. 3. Its broad substrate specificity is central to parallel biosynthesis of methoxylated and non-methoxylated alkaloids.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes with CpDCE to conversion of strictosidine aglycone into dihydrocorynantheal
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: downstream conversion of strictosidine aglycone to corynanthe-type intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms downstream intermediate toward quinine-related alkaloids and accepts methoxy/non-methoxy flux
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine-related methoxylated and non-methoxylated alkaloid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of both methoxy and non-methoxy dihydrocorynantheal-type products in pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-expression with upstream genes and substrate co-infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low downstream product accumulation without inclusion of transporter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used in heterologous pathway assembly because it tolerates methoxylated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported downstream enzyme; not newly discovered here but experimentally important in Results
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpDCS participates with CpDCE in generating dihydrocorynantheal from strictosidine aglycone. 2. Reconstitution data indicate it accepts both methoxylated and non-methoxylated substrates. 3. Its promiscuity contributes to parallel branch formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes with CpDCS to conversion of strictosidine aglycone into dihydrocorynantheal
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: downstream conversion of strictosidine aglycone to corynanthe-type intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms downstream intermediate toward quinine-related alkaloids and accepts methoxy/non-methoxy flux
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: quinine-related methoxylated and non-methoxylated alkaloid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: quinine is antimalarial; quinidine is antiarrhythmic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots and stem in C. pubescens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of both methoxy and non-methoxy dihydrocorynantheal-type products in pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-expression with upstream genes and substrate co-infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low downstream product accumulation without inclusion of transporter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used in heterologous pathway assembly because it tolerates methoxylated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported downstream enzyme; not newly discovered here but experimentally important in Results
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpDCE functions with CpDCS in generating dihydrocorynantheal-type intermediates. 2. Reconstitution showed downstream processing of both methoxylated and non-methoxylated intermediates. 3. It contributes to pathway promiscuity enabling parallel alkaloid production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces reactive strictosidine aglycone to tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: off-pathway branch from Cinchona alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux away from quinine-related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces strictosidine aglycone to off-pathway tetrahydroalstonine during reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional identification in N. benthamiana; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May siphon flux away from desired quinine-related products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important to consider or suppress in engineering if minimizing shunt products is desired
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpTHAS1 was identified as an off-pathway enzyme reducing strictosidine aglycone to tetrahydroalstonine. 2. It demonstrates competing branch chemistry in reconstitution. 3. It may reduce yield of desired Cinchona alkaloid products.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important to consider or suppress in engineering if minimizing shunt products is desired
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpTHAS2 was identified as an off-pathway enzyme reducing strictosidine aglycone to tetrahydroalstonine. 2. It demonstrates competing branch chemistry in reconstitution. 3. It may reduce yield of desired Cinchona alkaloid products.
